verses.text: The LORD empties the earth and turns it upside down, scattering its people; 'the land shall be utterly emptied.' Universal judgment is foretold. No rank or place escapes when God shakes the whole world.

The LORD empties the earth and turns it upside down, scattering its people; 'the land shall be utterly emptied.' Universal judgment is foretold. No rank or place escapes when God shakes the whole world.

The LORD empties the earth and turns it upside down, scattering its people; 'the land shall be utterly emptied.' Universal judgment is foretold. No rank or place escapes when God shakes the whole world.

The earth mourns and fades, defiled by its people who 'transgressed the laws... broken the everlasting covenant.' Sin pollutes the world itself. The curse falls because mankind has broken faith with God.

The earth mourns and fades, defiled by its people who 'transgressed the laws... broken the everlasting covenant.' Sin pollutes the world itself. The curse falls because mankind has broken faith with God.

The earth mourns and fades, defiled by its people who 'transgressed the laws... broken the everlasting covenant.' Sin pollutes the world itself. The curse falls because mankind has broken faith with God.
The wine fails, the merry are silenced, and music ceases; there is no more singing over the cup. Joy is taken from the earth. When God judges, even the world's revelry falls quiet.
The wine fails, the merry are silenced, and music ceases; there is no more singing over the cup. Joy is taken from the earth. When God judges, even the world's revelry falls quiet.
The wine fails, the merry are silenced, and music ceases; there is no more singing over the cup. Joy is taken from the earth. When God judges, even the world's revelry falls quiet.
The 'city of confusion' is broken down, its houses shut, and what remains is desolation, like the gleaning of an olive tree. Few are left. The proud city becomes an empty shell under God's hand.

Some lift their voices to sing, 'Glory to the righteous,' yet the prophet groans, 'My leanness, my leanness!' for the treacherous still deal treacherously. Praise and grief mingle. Even amid songs, sin's continuance pains the godly.

Fear, the pit, and the snare are on the earth; it reels 'like a drunkard,' for its transgression is heavy. The world staggers under judgment. Sin so weighs the earth that it totters like a falling hut.

Fear, the pit, and the snare are on the earth; it reels 'like a drunkard,' for its transgression is heavy. The world staggers under judgment. Sin so weighs the earth that it totters like a falling hut.

Fear, the pit, and the snare are on the earth; it reels 'like a drunkard,' for its transgression is heavy. The world staggers under judgment. Sin so weighs the earth that it totters like a falling hut.

The LORD will punish the powers of heaven and earth, and then 'the LORD of hosts shall reign in mount Zion... gloriously.' Judgment gives way to His reign. Beyond the world's collapse stands the everlasting throne of God.
